topic: OpenAI Codex Multi-Agent: Plugins, Persistent Memory & Multi-Day Agent Workflows

key_points: April 16, 2026 'Codex for (almost) everything' update ships 90+ plugins, multi-day agents, and cloud storage mounts
Plugins include Jira, full Microsoft 365 suite, Notion, Slack, HubSpot, Salesforce — no custom API setup required
Plugins authenticate via standard OAuth flows; IT/security teams should audit scopes and data-residency posture per connector
Multi-day agents can schedule future work and wake up automatically across days or weeks
Enterprise rollout with admin controls, memory governance, and audit logs coming soon
Codex subagents released March 16, 2026 — one manager agent coordinates multiple specialized coding agents
Each subagent runs in its own isolated cloud sandbox
spawn_agents_on_csv capability: reads CSV, spawns one worker per row, waits for batch, exports combined results
Up to 6 subagents running concurrently
Three built-in agent roles: explorer (read-only), worker (read-write), default (general)
Persistent memory retains context across sessions, user preferences, recurring workflows, tech stacks, project conventions
Scheduled automations let Codex run in the background — not just when computer is open
Cloud-based triggers enable Codex to start without manual user intervention
Treats Codex 'less like a one-off assistant and more like a teammate you configure and improve over time'
Codex usage doubled since GPT-5.2-Codex launch; 1M+ developers used Codex in past month
In April 2026, Codex moved to #1 due to GPT-5.5 material improvements in code quality and agentic execution
By Q1 2026, Claude Code overtook Copilot and Cursor in professional usage — fastest reversal in developer tooling history
GitHub Copilot offers most developed enterprise controls: org-wide policy, IP indemnification, audit logs
Multi-agent architectures now mainstream — Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot all support parallel sub-agents
Claude Code has strongest contextual awareness across entire codebase and best at coordinating parallel agents
Symphony framework — OpenAI's internal Elixir-based agent orchestration system — published to GitHub March 2026
Symphony is the same framework that powers Codex subagents internally
Codex powered by GPT-5.4 with 1M token context window, native computer use, and tool search
Custom agents configurable via TOML files in ~/.codex/agents/ or .codex/agents/
Subagents patterns now de facto industry standard across all major coding AI tools

stats.fact: Codex usage doubled since GPT-5.2-Codex launch in mid-December 2025
More than 1 million developers used Codex in the past month (as of April 2026)
84% of developers were using or planning to use AI in their workflows as of mid-2025 (StackOverflow survey)
90+ enterprise plugins shipped at the April 16, 2026 Codex update launch
Codex subagents support up to 6 concurrent agents per session
Codex pricing changed April 2, 2026 from per-message to API token usage-based; starts at $20/month on Plus plan
Codex moved to #1 ranking among AI coding agents in April 2026 following GPT-5.5 improvements
GitHub Copilot paused new individual sign-ups in Q2 2026 due to extreme compute demands from agentic /fleet features

angles: The Symphony framework: How OpenAI's internal Elixir-based orchestration system powers Codex subagents — and what enterprises can learn from it
AGENTS.md as the enterprise 'team rulebook': treating your AI coding agent like a configurable teammate rather than a one-off tool
Multi-day autonomous workflows vs. traditional CI/CD: Why scheduled Codex agents are poised to replace certain DevOps automation jobs
Security blind spots: The OAuth surface area problem when 90+ plugins connect to your corporate data stores
spawn_agents_on_csv for enterprise batch processing: a practical pattern for migrating large legacy codebases
Persistent memory governance: When does Codex memory become a liability in regulated industries?
The context window arms race: Codex's 1M token context vs. Claude Code's 1M token context — why architecture matters more than raw size
From coding assistant to autonomous software team: The philosophical shift required to deploy multi-agent Codex at scale
keywords: openai codex vs claude code enterprise 2026
